data.title: Matthews International to sell remaining businesses within SGK for $50M
data.description:
• Matthews International (NASDAQ:MATW) on Friday entered into a letter of intent (LOI) for the sale of the remaining operating businesses within the SGK Brand Solutions reporting segment, which primarily consists of its European roto-gravure packaging and surfaces businesses.
  • Under the terms of the LOI, Matthews will receive $50 million of total consideration, predominantly in cash which will be applied directly to the repayment of debt.
  • Earlier this month, Matthews entered into a definitive agreement under which it will receive $350 million of total upfront consideration in connection with the sale of its interest in SGK.
  • “The sale of the remaining operating businesses within SGK is another step forward toward a more streamlined business structure and further evidence of our commitment to unlocking the value of our businesses,” said Joseph Bartolacci, Chief Executive Officer of Matthews. “This transaction provides us with additional cash to continue paying down debt. When combined with the sale of these remaining European operating businesses within the SGK reporting segment, the total upfront consideration for the sale of SGK will be $400 million, implying a total current value of the segment in excess of $600 million. Once the new entity of our previously announced transaction is fully synergized and we dispose of our investment in our operating software, we expect the total realized value of the divestiture of this segment to exceed $700 million.”
  • The transaction is expected to be completed in the third quarter, the company said.
  • MATW +4.08% premarket to $25.99.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: WeRide jumps after Nvidia discloses position
data.description:
WeRide (WDR) shares jumped over 107% premarket on Friday after AI giant Nvidia (NVDA) disclosed a position in the Chinese robotaxi developer.

As of Dec. 31, 2024, Nvidia's (NVDA) holdings in WeRide (WDR) totaled around 1.74M shares, worth around $24.65M, according to the chip giant’s latest 13F filing.

WeRide, which makes robobuses, robotaxis and robosweepers and related sensor suites, was valued at about $4.4B in a March 2022 fundraising round.

The company filed for U.S. IPO in August 2024. It made its Nasdaq debut in late October, after raising $440.5M in the IPO and a private placement.

data.title: Stock futures dip marginally, but Wall Street set to snap two-week losing streak
data.description:
U.S. stock index futures were slightly lower on Friday, but Wall Street was on track to snap a two-week losing streak. The past few days were dominated by President Donald Trump's remarks on tariffs and by economic data on inflation.

Benchmark S&P futures (SPX) were -0.14% early in the morning, while Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) were -0.24%. Dow futures (INDU) were -0.27%.

Tariffs took center stage this week, with Trump on Monday announcing 25% levies on all steel and aluminum imports into the U.S. On Thursday, Trump signed a memorandum that tasked his administration to investigate current trade relations and propose reciprocal tariffs on a country-specific basis.

Still, the fact that Trump did not immediately put in effect the tit-for-tat tariffs and was scant on details, helped market sentiment and drove Wall Street more than 1% higher in Thursday's session. A favorable producer inflation report for January also helped.

On the surface, the data showed that the M/M increase in the headline producer price index (PPI) was higher than expectations, while the core PPI came in-line. However, digging deeper, the components in the PPI that directly feed into the core personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index came in soft for January. That bodes well for the Federal Reserve's favorite inflation gauge.

The PPI update helped overcome the shock provided by hotter-than-expected consumer price index (CPI) readings on Wednesday.

"It was a love-in for markets yesterday with bonds and equities rallying following better-than-expected inflation data and news that details on reciprocal tariffs would not come before April. This led to a revival of hopes that the Fed would still cut rates this year, particularly after the more hawkish fears post the upside surprise in the CPI report the previous day," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

Treasury yields were mixed on Friday after two straight roller-coaster sessions. The 10-year yield (US10Y) was little changed at 4.54%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was down nearly 2 basis points to 4.31%.

Investors will receive the January retail sales report in just over an hour. After the closing bell, 13F filings will be in focus - regulatory disclosures by major funds of their quarterly equity ownership changes.

data.title: Moderna swings to Q4 loss after nearly 66% drop in revenue
data.description:
Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) shares fell ~5% in the premarket on Friday after the COVID-19 vaccine maker, with its Q4 2024 results, topped the consensus for revenue but missed Street forecasts for earnings following a ~66% YoY contraction in its topline.

The Cambridge, Massachusetts-based biotech reported $966M in revenue for the quarter compared to $2.8B in the prior-year period, driven by a sharp decline in demand for its COVID-19 vaccine, Spikevax, as expected.

Sales from Spikevax reached $923M in Q4, while mRESVIA, MRNA’s vaccine for respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) added $15M to the topline, expanding their full-year revenue contributions to $3.1B and $25M, respectively.

The company attributed the sharp revenue decline to the earlier-than-anticipated launch of its updated COVID-19 vaccine in the U.S., which it said had shifted sales into the third quarter.

However, MRNA’s operating expenses fell only ~22% YoY to $2.2B in Q4, including a non-cash charge of $238M related to the termination of a contract manufacturing deal, as the company recorded a net loss of $1.1B during the quarter compared to a net income of $217M in Q4 2023.

Meanwhile, MRNA’s full-year revenue plunged ~53% YoY to $3.2B while its net loss stood at $3.6B, down ~25% YoY from a year ago, as its cost of sales as a percentage of net product sales reached 47% from 70% in 2023, driven by strategic cost initiatives launched in Q3 2023.

Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) reaffirmed its full-year outlook announced last month, indicating $1.5B - $2.5B in revenue, including roughly $0.2B of revenue anticipated in H1 2025, given the seasonality of its respiratory business.

As for liquidity, Moderna’s (NASDAQ:MRNA) cash, cash equivalents, and investments also fell ~18% YoY in 2024 to $7.0B, and the company projected its year-end cash and investments to reach approximately $6B in 2025.
